{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q838856]] Wikidata name: 1st day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|March 1|qq=March 1}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初一|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 1일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 1''' is the 1st day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜}}.
== Events == * [[:ja:敏達天皇|敏達天皇]] 14th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:585|585]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – In response to an appeal by the anti-Buddhist advocate [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]], who claimed that the spread of Buddhism was the cause of an epidemic, the court reported this to the emperor. * [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 15th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1587|1587]] [[:en:April 8|April 8]]) – [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] set out with 25,000 troops to subjugate the Shimazu clan in Kyushu. * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1834|1834]] [[:en:April 9|April 9]]) – [[:en:Mizuno Tadakuni|Mizuno Tadakuni]] assumed the office of [[:en:Rōjū|Rōjū]], implementing the Tenpō reforms. * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:April 20|April 20]]) – Post offices (now known as [[:en:Post office|Yūbin-kyoku]]) were established in Tokyo, Kyoto, and Osaka, marking the beginning of postal services (recognized as Postal Memorial Day).
== Births == * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 12nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1829|1829]] [[:en:April 4|April 4]]) – [[:en:Inoue Genzaburō|Inoue Genzaburō]], a member of the Shinsengumi (died [[:en:1868|1868]]). * [[:en:Genji era|Genji]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1864|1864]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]]) – {{ill|Wakamatsu Sentako|ja|若松賎子|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamatsu Sentako|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若松賤子}}, a translator (died [[:en:1896|1896]]). * [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1867|1867]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – {{ill|Mutō Yamaichi|ja|武藤山治|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutō Yamaichi|qq=}}, a businessperson and manager of the Kanebo conglomerate (died [[:en:1934|1934]]). * Meiji 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1867|1867]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – {{ill|Shimura Gentarō|ja|志村源太郎|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimura Gentarō|qq=}}, a banker and president of the Nippon Kangyo Bank (died [[:en:1930|1930]]).
== Deaths == * [[:ja:欽明天皇|欽明天皇]] 32nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:570|570]] [[:en:March 22|March 22]]) – [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga Iname]], a great minister (died ?; birth year may be around [[:en:506|506]]). * [[:ja:弘仁|弘仁]] 8th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:817|817]] [[:en:March 21|March 21]]) – {{ill|Katsumichi|ja|勝道|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsumichi|qq=}}, the founder of Nikko-san (born [[:en:735|735]]).
== Commemorations == * [[:ja:御船祭|御船祭]] – A festival held in Toba, Mie Prefecture, celebrating the sacred boat ritual.

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q838856]] Wikidata name: 1st day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|March 1|qq=March 1}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初一|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 1일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 1''' is the 1st day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:ja:敏達天皇|敏達天皇]] 14th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:585|585]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – In response to an appeal by the anti-Buddhist advocate [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]], who claimed that the spread of Buddhism was the cause of an epidemic, the court reported this to the emperor. * [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 15th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1587|1587]] [[:en:April 8|April 8]]) – [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] set out with 25,000 troops to subjugate the Shimazu clan in Kyushu. * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1834|1834]] [[:en:April 9|April 9]]) – [[:en:Mizuno Tadakuni|Mizuno Tadakuni]] assumed the office of [[:en:Rōjū|Rōjū]], implementing the Tenpō reforms. * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:April 20|April 20]]) – Post offices (now known as [[:en:Post office|Yūbin-kyoku]]) were established in Tokyo, Kyoto, and Osaka, marking the beginning of postal services (recognized as Postal Memorial Day).
== Births == * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 12nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1829|1829]] [[:en:April 4|April 4]]) – [[:en:Inoue Genzaburō|Inoue Genzaburō]], a member of the Shinsengumi (died [[:en:1868|1868]]). * [[:en:Genji era|Genji]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1864|1864]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]]) – {{ill|Wakamatsu Sentako|ja|若松賎子|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamatsu Sentako|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若松賤子|1=Wakamatsu Shizuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q535871}}, a translator (died [[:en:1896|1896]]). * [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1867|1867]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – {{ill|Mutō Yamaichi|ja|武藤山治|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutō Yamaichi|qq=|1=Mutō Yamaichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23998342}}, a businessperson and manager of the Kanebo conglomerate (died [[:en:1934|1934]]). * Meiji 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1867|1867]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – {{ill|Shimura Gentarō|ja|志村源太郎|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimura Gentarō|qq=|1=Shimura Gentarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21699960}}, a banker and president of the Nippon Kangyo Bank (died [[:en:1930|1930]]).
== Deaths == * [[:ja:欽明天皇|欽明天皇]] 32nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:570|570]] [[:en:March 22|March 22]]) – [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga Iname]], a great minister (died ?; birth year may be around [[:en:506|506]]). * [[:ja:弘仁|弘仁]] 8th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:817|817]] [[:en:March 21|March 21]]) – {{ill|Katsumichi|ja|勝道|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsumichi|qq=|1=Shōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400506}}, the founder of Nikko-san (born [[:en:735|735]]).
== Commemorations == * [[:ja:御船祭|御船祭]] – A festival held in Toba, Mie Prefecture, celebrating the sacred boat ritual.

== Births == * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 12nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1829|1829]] [[:en:April 4|April 4]]) – [[:en:Inoue Genzaburō|Inoue Genzaburō]], a member of the Shinsengumi (died [[:en:1868|1868]]). * [[:en:Genji era|Genji]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1864|1864]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]]) – {{ill|Wakamatsu Sentako|ja|若松賎子|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamatsu Sentako|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若松賤子|1=Wakamatsu Shizuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q535871}}, a translator (died [[:en:1896|1896]]). * [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1867|1867]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – {{ill|Mutō Yamaichi|ja|武藤山治|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutō Yamaichi|qq=|1=Mutō Yamaichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23998342}}, a businessperson and manager of the Kanebo conglomerate (died [[:en:1934|1934]]). * Meiji 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1867|1867]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – {{ill|Shimura Gentarō|ja|志村源太郎|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimura Gentarō|qq=|1=Shimura Gentarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21699960}}, a banker and president of the Nippon Kangyo Bank (died [[:en:1930|1930]]).
== Deaths == * [[:ja:欽明天皇|欽明天皇]] 32nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:570|570]] [[:en:March 22|March 22]]) – [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga Iname]], a great minister (died ?; birth year may be around [[:en:506|506]]). * [[:ja:弘仁|弘仁]] 8th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:817|817]] [[:en:March 21|March 21]]) – {{ill|Katsumichi|ja|勝道|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsumichi|qq=|1=Shōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400506}}, the founder of Nikko-san (born [[:en:735|735]]).
== Commemorations == * [[:ja:御船祭|御船祭]] – A festival held in Toba, Mie Prefecture, celebrating the sacred boat ritual.

== Births == * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 12nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1829|1829]] [[:en:April 4|April 4]]) – [[:en:Inoue Genzaburō|Inoue Genzaburō]], a member of the Shinsengumi (died [[:en:1868|1868]]). * [[:en:Genji era|Genji]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1864|1864]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]]) – {{ill|Wakamatsu Sentako|ja|若松賎子|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamatsu Sentako|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若松賤子|1=Wakamatsu Shizuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q535871}}, a translator (died [[:en:1896|1896]]). * [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1867|1867]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – {{ill|Mutō Yamaichi|ja|武藤山治|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutō Yamaichi|qq=|1=Mutō Yamaichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23998342}}, a businessperson and manager of the Kanebo conglomerate (died [[:en:1934|1934]]). * Meiji 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1867|1867]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – {{ill|Shimura Gentarō|ja|志村源太郎|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimura Gentarō|qq=|1=Shimura Gentarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21699960}}, a banker and president of the Nippon Kangyo Bank (died [[:en:1930|1930]]).
== Deaths == * [[:ja:欽明天皇|欽明天皇]] 32nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:570|570]] [[:en:March 22|March 22]]) – [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga Iname]], a great minister (died ?; birth year may be around [[:en:506|506]]). * [[:ja:弘仁|弘仁]] 8th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:817|817]] [[:en:March 21|March 21]]) – {{ill|Katsumichi|ja|勝道|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsumichi|qq=|1=Shōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400506}}, the founder of Nikko-san (born [[:en:735|735]]).
== Commemorations == * [[:ja:御船祭|御船祭]] – A festival held in Toba, Mie Prefecture, celebrating the sacred boat ritual.
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|2|30|3|2|2|1|4|3|0301|qq=}}

== Births == * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 12nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1829|1829]] [[:en:April 4|April 4]]) – [[:en:Inoue Genzaburō|Inoue Genzaburō]], a member of the Shinsengumi (died [[:en:1868|1868]]). * [[:en:Genji era|Genji]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1864|1864]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]]) – {{ill|Wakamatsu Sentako|ja|若松賎子|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamatsu Sentako|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若松賤子|1=Wakamatsu Shizuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q535871}}, a translator (died [[:en:1896|1896]]). * [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1867|1867]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – {{ill|Mutō Yamaichi|ja|武藤山治|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutō Yamaichi|qq=|1=Mutō Yamaichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23998342}}, a businessperson and manager of the Kanebo conglomerate (died [[:en:1934|1934]]). * Meiji 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1867|1867]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – {{ill|Shimura Gentarō|ja|志村源太郎|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimura Gentarō|qq=|1=Shimura Gentarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21699960}}, a banker and president of the Nippon Kangyo Bank (died [[:en:1930|1930]]).
== Deaths == * [[:ja:欽明天皇|欽明天皇]] 32nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:570|570]] [[:en:March 22|March 22]]) – [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga Iname]], a great minister (died ?; birth year may be around [[:en:506|506]]). * [[:ja:弘仁|弘仁]] 8th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:817|817]] [[:en:March 21|March 21]]) – {{ill|Katsumichi|ja|勝道|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsumichi|qq=|1=Shōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400506}}, the founder of Nikko-san (born [[:en:735|735]]).
== Commemorations == * [[:ja:御船祭|御船祭]] – A festival held in Toba, Mie Prefecture, celebrating the sacred boat ritual.

== Births == * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 12nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1829|1829]] [[:en:April 4|April 4]]) – [[:en:Inoue Genzaburō|Inoue Genzaburō]], a member of the Shinsengumi (died [[:en:1868|1868]]). * [[:en:Genji era|Genji]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1864|1864]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]]) – {{ill|Wakamatsu Sentako|ja|若松賎子|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamatsu Sentako|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若松賤子|1=Wakamatsu Shizuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q535871}}, a translator (died [[:en:1896|1896]]). * [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1867|1867]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – {{ill|Mutō Yamaichi|ja|武藤山治|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutō Yamaichi|qq=|1=Mutō Yamaichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23998342}}, a businessperson and manager of the Kanebo conglomerate (died [[:en:1934|1934]]). * Meiji 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1867|1867]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – {{ill|Shimura Gentarō|ja|志村源太郎|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimura Gentarō|qq=|1=Shimura Gentarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21699960}}, a banker and president of the Nippon Kangyo Bank (died [[:en:1930|1930]]).
== Deaths == * [[:ja:欽明天皇|欽明天皇]] 32nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:570|570]] [[:en:March 22|March 22]]) – [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga Iname]], a great minister (died ?; birth year may be around [[:en:506|506]]). * [[:ja:弘仁|弘仁]] 8th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:817|817]] [[:en:March 21|March 21]]) – {{ill|Katsumichi|ja|勝道|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsumichi|qq=|1=Shōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400506}}, the founder of Nikko-san (born [[:en:735|735]]).
== Commemorations == * [[:ja:御船祭|御船祭]] – A festival held in Toba, Mie Prefecture, celebrating the sacred boat ritual.

== Births == * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 12nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1829|1829]] [[:en:April 4|April 4]]) – [[:en:Inoue Genzaburō|Inoue Genzaburō]], a member of the Shinsengumi (died [[:en:1868|1868]]). * [[:en:Genji era|Genji]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1864|1864]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]]) – {{ill|Wakamatsu Sentako|ja|若松賎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamatsu Sentako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若松賤子|1=Wakamatsu Shizuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q535871}}, a translator (died [[:en:1896|1896]]). * [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1867|1867]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – {{ill|Mutō Yamaichi|ja|武藤山治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutō Yamaichi|1=Mutō Yamaichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23998342}}, a businessperson and manager of the Kanebo conglomerate (died [[:en:1934|1934]]). * Meiji 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1867|1867]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – {{ill|Shimura Gentarō|ja|志村源太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimura Gentarō|1=Shimura Gentarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21699960}}, a banker and president of the Nippon Kangyo Bank (died [[:en:1930|1930]]).
== Deaths == * [[:ja:欽明天皇|欽明天皇]] 32nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:570|570]] [[:en:March 22|March 22]]) – [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga Iname]], a great minister (died ?; birth year may be around [[:en:506|506]]). * [[:ja:弘仁|弘仁]] 8th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:817|817]] [[:en:March 21|March 21]]) – {{ill|Katsumichi|ja|勝道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsumichi|1=Shōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400506}}, the founder of Nikko-san (born [[:en:735|735]]).
== Commemorations == * [[:ja:御船祭|御船祭]] – A festival held in Toba, Mie Prefecture, celebrating the sacred boat ritual.

== Births == * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 12nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1829|1829]] [[:en:April 4|April 4]]) – [[:en:Inoue Genzaburō|Inoue Genzaburō]], a member of the Shinsengumi (died [[:en:1868|1868]]). * [[:en:Genji era|Genji]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1864|1864]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]]) – {{ill|Wakamatsu Sentako|ja|若松賎子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamatsu Sentako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若松賤子|1=Wakamatsu Shizuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q535871}}, a translator (died [[:en:1896|1896]]). * [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1867|1867]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – {{ill|Mutō Yamaichi|ja|武藤山治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutō Yamaichi|1=Mutō Yamaichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23998342}}, a businessperson and manager of the Kanebo conglomerate (died [[:en:1934|1934]]). * Meiji 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1867|1867]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – {{ill|Shimura Gentarō|ja|志村源太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimura Gentarō|1=Shimura Gentarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21699960}}, a banker and president of the Nippon Kangyo Bank (died [[:en:1930|1930]]).
== Deaths == * [[:ja:欽明天皇|欽明天皇]] 32nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:570|570]] [[:en:March 22|March 22]]) – [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga Iname]], a great minister (died ?; birth year may be around [[:en:506|506]]). * [[:ja:弘仁|弘仁]] 8th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:817|817]] [[:en:March 21|March 21]]) – {{ill|Katsumichi|ja|勝道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsumichi|1=Shōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400506}}, the founder of Nikko-san (born [[:en:735|735]]).
== Commemorations == * [[:ja:御船祭|御船祭]] – A festival held in Toba, Mie Prefecture, celebrating the sacred boat ritual.

== Births == * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 12nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1829|1829]] [[:en:April 4|April 4]]) – [[:en:Inoue Genzaburō|Inoue Genzaburō]], a member of the Shinsengumi (died [[:en:1868|1868]]). * [[:en:Genji era|Genji]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1864|1864]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]]) – {{ill|Wakamatsu Sentako|ja|若松賎子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamatsu Sentako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若松賤子|1=Wakamatsu Shizuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q535871}}, a translator (died [[:en:1896|1896]]). * [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1867|1867]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – {{ill|Mutō Yamaichi|ja|武藤山治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutō Yamaichi|1=Mutō Yamaichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23998342}}, a businessperson and manager of the Kanebo conglomerate (died [[:en:1934|1934]]). * Meiji 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1867|1867]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – {{ill|Shimura Gentarō|ja|志村源太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimura Gentarō|1=Shimura Gentarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21699960}}, a banker and president of the Nippon Kangyo Bank (died [[:en:1930|1930]]).
== Deaths == * [[:ja:欽明天皇|欽明天皇]] 32nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:570|570]] [[:en:March 22|March 22]]) – [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga Iname]], a great minister (died ?; birth year may be around [[:en:506|506]]). * [[:ja:弘仁|弘仁]] 8th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:817|817]] [[:en:March 21|March 21]]) – {{ill|Katsumichi|ja|勝道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsumichi|1=Shōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400506}}, the founder of Nikko-san (born [[:en:735|735]]).
== Commemorations == * [[:ja:御船祭|御船祭]] – A festival held in Toba, Mie Prefecture, celebrating the sacred boat ritual.

== Births == * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 12nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1829|1829]] [[:en:April 4|April 4]]) – [[:en:Inoue Genzaburō|Inoue Genzaburō]], a member of the Shinsengumi (died [[:en:1868|1868]]). * [[:en:Genji era|Genji]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1864|1864]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]]) – {{ill|Wakamatsu Sentako|ja|若松賎子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamatsu Sentako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若松賤子|1=Wakamatsu Shizuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q535871}}, a translator (died [[:en:1896|1896]]). * [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1867|1867]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – {{ill|Mutō Yamaichi|ja|武藤山治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutō Yamaichi|1=Mutō Yamaichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23998342}}, a businessperson and manager of the Kanebo conglomerate (died [[:en:1934|1934]]). * Meiji 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1867|1867]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – {{ill|Shimura Gentarō|ja|志村源太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimura Gentarō|1=Shimura Gentarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21699960}}, a banker and president of the Nippon Kangyo Bank (died [[:en:1930|1930]]).
== Deaths == * [[:ja:欽明天皇|欽明天皇]] 32nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:570|570]] [[:en:March 22|March 22]]) – [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga Iname]], a great minister (died ?; birth year may be around [[:en:506|506]]). * [[:ja:弘仁|弘仁]] 8th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:817|817]] [[:en:March 21|March 21]]) – {{ill|Katsumichi|ja|勝道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsumichi|1=Shōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400506}}, the founder of Nikko-san (born [[:en:735|735]]).
== Commemorations == * [[:ja:御船祭|御船祭]] – A festival held in Toba, Mie Prefecture, celebrating the sacred boat ritual.

== Births == * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 12nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1829|1829]] [[:en:April 4|April 4]]) – [[:en:Inoue Genzaburō|Inoue Genzaburō]], a member of the Shinsengumi (died [[:en:1868|1868]]). * [[:en:Genji era|Genji]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1864|1864]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]]) – {{ill|Wakamatsu Sentako|ja|若松賎子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamatsu Sentako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若松賤子|1=Wakamatsu Shizuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q535871}}, a translator (died [[:en:1896|1896]]). * [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1867|1867]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – {{ill|Mutō Yamaichi|ja|武藤山治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutō Yamaichi|1=Mutō Yamaichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23998342}}, a businessperson and manager of the Kanebo conglomerate (died [[:en:1934|1934]]). * Meiji 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1867|1867]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – {{ill|Shimura Gentarō|ja|志村源太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimura Gentarō|1=Shimura Gentarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21699960}}, a banker and president of the Nippon Kangyo Bank (died [[:en:1930|1930]]).
== Deaths == * [[:ja:欽明天皇|欽明天皇]] 32nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:570|570]] [[:en:March 22|March 22]]) – [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga Iname]], a great minister (died ?; birth year may be around [[:en:506|506]]). * [[:ja:弘仁|弘仁]] 8th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:817|817]] [[:en:March 21|March 21]]) – {{ill|Katsumichi|ja|勝道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsumichi|1=Shōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400506}}, the founder of Nikko-san (born [[:en:735|735]]).
== Commemorations == * [[:ja:御船祭|御船祭]] – A festival held in Toba, Mie Prefecture, celebrating the sacred boat ritual.
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|2|30|3|2|2|1|4|3|0301|qq=}}

== Events == * [[:ja:敏達天皇|敏達天皇]] 14th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:585|585]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – In response to an appeal by the anti-Buddhist advocate [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]], who claimed that the spread of Buddhism was the cause of an epidemic, the court reported this to the emperor. * [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 15th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1587|1587]] [[:en:April 8|April 8]]) – [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] set out with 25,000 troops to subjugate the Shimazu clan in Kyushu. * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1834|1834]] [[:en:April 9|April 9]]) – [[:en:Mizuno Tadakuni|Mizuno Tadakuni]] assumed the office of [[:en:Rōjū|Rōjū]], implementing the Tenpō reforms. * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:April 20|April 20]]) – Post offices (now known as [[:en:Post office|Yūbin-kyoku]]) were established in Tokyo, Kyoto, and Osaka, marking the beginning of postal services (recognized as Postal Memorial Day).
== Births == * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 12nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1829|1829]] [[:en:April 4|April 4]]) – [[:en:Inoue Genzaburō|Inoue Genzaburō]], a member of the Shinsengumi (died [[:en:1868|1868]]). * [[:en:Genji era|Genji]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1864|1864]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]]) – {{ill|Wakamatsu Sentako|ja|若松賎子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamatsu Sentako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若松賤子|1=Wakamatsu Shizuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q535871}}, a translator (died [[:en:1896|1896]]). * [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1867|1867]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – {{ill|Mutō Yamaichi|ja|武藤山治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutō Yamaichi|1=Mutō Yamaichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23998342}}, a businessperson and manager of the Kanebo conglomerate (died [[:en:1934|1934]]). * Meiji 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1867|1867]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – {{ill|Shimura Gentarō|ja|志村源太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimura Gentarō|1=Shimura Gentarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21699960}}, a banker and president of the Nippon Kangyo Bank (died [[:en:1930|1930]]).
== Deaths == * [[:ja:欽明天皇|欽明天皇]] 32nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:570|570]] [[:en:March 22|March 22]]) – [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga Iname]], a great minister (died ?; birth year may be around [[:en:506|506]]). * [[:ja:弘仁|弘仁]] 8th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:817|817]] [[:en:March 21|March 21]]) – {{ill|Katsumichi|ja|勝道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsumichi|1=Shōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400506}}, the founder of Nikko-san (born [[:en:735|735]]).
== Commemorations == * [[:ja:御船祭|御船祭]] – A festival held in Toba, Mie Prefecture, celebrating the sacred boat ritual.

== Events == * [[:ja:敏達天皇|敏達天皇]] 14th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:585|585]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – In response to an appeal by the anti-Buddhist advocate [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]], who claimed that the spread of Buddhism was the cause of an epidemic, the court reported this to the emperor. * [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 15th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1587|1587]] [[:en:April 8|April 8]]) – [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] set out with 25,000 troops to subjugate the Shimazu clan in Kyushu. * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1834|1834]] [[:en:April 9|April 9]]) – [[:en:Mizuno Tadakuni|Mizuno Tadakuni]] assumed the office of [[:en:Rōjū|Rōjū]], implementing the Tenpō reforms. * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:April 20|April 20]]) – Post offices (now known as [[:en:Post office|Yūbin-kyoku]]) were established in Tokyo, Kyoto, and Osaka, marking the beginning of postal services (recognized as Postal Memorial Day).
== Births == * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 12nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1829|1829]] [[:en:April 4|April 4]]) – [[:en:Inoue Genzaburō|Inoue Genzaburō]], a member of the Shinsengumi (died [[:en:1868|1868]]). * [[:en:Genji era|Genji]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1864|1864]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]]) – {{ill|Wakamatsu Sentako|ja|若松賎子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamatsu Sentako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若松賤子|1=Wakamatsu Shizuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q535871}}, a translator (died [[:en:1896|1896]]). * [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1867|1867]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – {{ill|Mutō Yamaichi|ja|武藤山治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutō Yamaichi|1=Mutō Yamaichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23998342}}, a businessperson and manager of the Kanebo conglomerate (died [[:en:1934|1934]]). * Meiji 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1867|1867]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – {{ill|Shimura Gentarō|ja|志村源太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimura Gentarō|1=Shimura Gentarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21699960}}, a banker and president of the Nippon Kangyo Bank (died [[:en:1930|1930]]).
== Deaths == * [[:ja:欽明天皇|欽明天皇]] 32nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:570|570]] [[:en:March 22|March 22]]) – [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga Iname]], a great minister (died ?; birth year may be around [[:en:506|506]]). * [[:ja:弘仁|弘仁]] 8th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:817|817]] [[:en:March 21|March 21]]) – {{ill|Katsumichi|ja|勝道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsumichi|1=Shōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400506}}, the founder of Nikko-san (born [[:en:735|735]]).
== Commemorations == * [[:ja:御船祭|御船祭]] – A festival held in Toba, Mie Prefecture, celebrating the sacred boat ritual.
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|2|30|3|2|2|1|4|3|0301|qq=}}

Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:ja:敏達天皇|敏達天皇]] 14th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|585|en|585|WD=Q32201|lt=585}} {{ill|April 5|en|April 5|WD=Q2507|lt=April 5}}) – In response to an appeal by the anti-Buddhist advocate {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya}}, who claimed that the spread of Buddhism was the cause of an epidemic, the court reported this to the emperor. * {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} 15th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1587|en|1587|WD=Q6744|lt=1587}} {{ill|April 8|en|April 8|WD=Q2503|lt=April 8}}) – {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} set out with 25,000 troops to subjugate the Shimazu clan in Kyushu. * {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō}} 5th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1834|en|1834|WD=Q7591|lt=1834}} {{ill|April 9|en|April 9|WD=Q2504|lt=April 9}}) – {{ill|Mizuno Tadakuni|en|Mizuno Tadakuni|WD=Q2975848|lt=Mizuno Tadakuni}} assumed the office of {{ill|Rōjū|en|Rōjū|WD=Q1196812|lt=Rōjū}}, implementing the Tenpō reforms. * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji}} 4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871}} {{ill|April 20|en|April 20|WD=Q2524|lt=April 20}}) – Post offices (now known as {{ill|Post office|en|Post office|WD=Q35054|lt=Yūbin-kyoku}}) were established in Tokyo, Kyoto, and Osaka, marking the beginning of postal services (recognized as Postal Memorial Day).
== Births == * {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei}} 12nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1829|en|1829|WD=Q7572|lt=1829}} {{ill|April 4|en|April 4|WD=Q2508|lt=April 4}}) – {{ill|Inoue Genzaburō|en|Inoue Genzaburō|WD=Q1039195|lt=Inoue Genzaburō}}, a member of the Shinsengumi (died {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868}}). * {{ill|Genji era|en|Genji era|WD=Q1325419|lt=Genji}} 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1864|en|1864|WD=Q7704|lt=1864}} {{ill|April 6|en|April 6|WD=Q2506|lt=April 6}}) – {{ill|Wakamatsu Sentako|ja|若松賎子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamatsu Sentako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若松賤子|1=Wakamatsu Shizuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q535871}}, a translator (died {{ill|1896|en|1896|WD=Q7846|lt=1896}}). * {{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1867|en|1867|WD=Q7715|lt=1867}} {{ill|April 5|en|April 5|WD=Q2507|lt=April 5}}) – {{ill|Mutō Yamaichi|ja|武藤山治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutō Yamaichi|1=Mutō Yamaichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23998342}}, a businessperson and manager of the Kanebo conglomerate (died {{ill|1934|en|1934|WD=Q18714|lt=1934}}). * Meiji 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1867|en|1867|WD=Q7715|lt=1867}} {{ill|April 5|en|April 5|WD=Q2507|lt=April 5}}) – {{ill|Shimura Gentarō|ja|志村源太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimura Gentarō|1=Shimura Gentarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21699960}}, a banker and president of the Nippon Kangyo Bank (died {{ill|1930|en|1930|WD=Q18787|lt=1930}}).
== Deaths == * [[:ja:欽明天皇|欽明天皇]] 32nd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|570|en|570|WD=Q23885|lt=570}} {{ill|March 22|en|March 22|WD=Q2435|lt=March 22}}) – {{ill|Soga no Iname|en|Soga no Iname|WD=Q595897|lt=Soga Iname}}, a great minister (died ?; birth year may be around {{ill|506|en|506|WD=Q32909|lt=506}}). * [[:ja:弘仁|弘仁]] 8th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|817|en|817|WD=Q25030|lt=817}} {{ill|March 21|en|March 21|WD=Q2433|lt=March 21}}) – {{ill|Katsumichi|ja|勝道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsumichi|1=Shōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400506}}, the founder of Nikko-san (born {{ill|735|en|735|WD=Q23576|lt=735}}).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 1 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月1日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 1일]][[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 1 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:三月初一]][[zh:三月初一]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 1 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月1日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 1일]][[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 1 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:三月初一]][[zh:三月初一]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 1 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月1日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 1일]][[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 1 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:三月初一]][[zh:三月初一]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q838856]] Wikidata name: 1st day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|March 1|qq=March 1}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初一|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 1일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 1''' is the 1st day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:ja:敏達天皇|敏達天皇]] 14th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|585|en|585|WD=Q32201|lt=585}} {{ill|April 5|en|April 5|WD=Q2507|lt=April 5}}) – In response to an appeal by the anti-Buddhist advocate {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya}}, who claimed that the spread of Buddhism was the cause of an epidemic, the court reported this to the emperor. * {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} 15th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1587|en|1587|WD=Q6744|lt=1587}} {{ill|April 8|en|April 8|WD=Q2503|lt=April 8}}) – {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} set out with 25,000 troops to subjugate the Shimazu clan in Kyushu. * {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō}} 5th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1834|en|1834|WD=Q7591|lt=1834}} {{ill|April 9|en|April 9|WD=Q2504|lt=April 9}}) – {{ill|Mizuno Tadakuni|en|Mizuno Tadakuni|WD=Q2975848|lt=Mizuno Tadakuni}} assumed the office of {{ill|Rōjū|en|Rōjū|WD=Q1196812|lt=Rōjū}}, implementing the Tenpō reforms. * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji}} 4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871}} {{ill|April 20|en|April 20|WD=Q2524|lt=April 20}}) – Post offices (now known as {{ill|Post office|en|Post office|WD=Q35054|lt=Yūbin-kyoku}}) were established in Tokyo, Kyoto, and Osaka, marking the beginning of postal services (recognized as Postal Memorial Day).
== Births == * {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei}} 12nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1829|en|1829|WD=Q7572|lt=1829}} {{ill|April 4|en|April 4|WD=Q2508|lt=April 4}}) – {{ill|Inoue Genzaburō|en|Inoue Genzaburō|WD=Q1039195|lt=Inoue Genzaburō}}, a member of the Shinsengumi (died {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868}}). * {{ill|Genji era|en|Genji era|WD=Q1325419|lt=Genji}} 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1864|en|1864|WD=Q7704|lt=1864}} {{ill|April 6|en|April 6|WD=Q2506|lt=April 6}}) – {{ill|Wakamatsu Sentako|ja|若松賎子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamatsu Sentako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若松賤子|1=Wakamatsu Shizuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q535871}}, a translator (died {{ill|1896|en|1896|WD=Q7846|lt=1896}}). * {{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1867|en|1867|WD=Q7715|lt=1867}} {{ill|April 5|en|April 5|WD=Q2507|lt=April 5}}) – {{ill|Mutō Yamaichi|ja|武藤山治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutō Yamaichi|1=Mutō Yamaichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23998342}}, a businessperson and manager of the Kanebo conglomerate (died {{ill|1934|en|1934|WD=Q18714|lt=1934}}). * Meiji 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1867|en|1867|WD=Q7715|lt=1867}} {{ill|April 5|en|April 5|WD=Q2507|lt=April 5}}) – {{ill|Shimura Gentarō|ja|志村源太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimura Gentarō|1=Shimura Gentarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21699960}}, a banker and president of the Nippon Kangyo Bank (died {{ill|1930|en|1930|WD=Q18787|lt=1930}}).
== Deaths == * [[:ja:欽明天皇|欽明天皇]] 32nd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|570|en|570|WD=Q23885|lt=570}} {{ill|March 22|en|March 22|WD=Q2435|lt=March 22}}) – {{ill|Soga no Iname|en|Soga no Iname|WD=Q595897|lt=Soga Iname}}, a great minister (died ?; birth year may be around {{ill|506|en|506|WD=Q32909|lt=506}}). * [[:ja:弘仁|弘仁]] 8th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|817|en|817|WD=Q25030|lt=817}} {{ill|March 21|en|March 21|WD=Q2433|lt=March 21}}) – {{ill|Katsumichi|ja|勝道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsumichi|1=Shōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400506}}, the founder of Nikko-san (born {{ill|735|en|735|WD=Q23576|lt=735}}).
== Commemorations == * [[:ja:御船祭|御船祭]] – A festival held in Toba, Mie Prefecture, celebrating the sacred boat ritual.
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|2|30|3|2|2|1|4|3|0301|qq=}}

Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:ja:敏達天皇|敏達天皇]] 14th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|585|en|585|WD=Q32201|lt=585}} {{ill|April 5|en|April 5|WD=Q2507|lt=April 5}}) – In response to an appeal by the anti-Buddhist advocate {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya}}, who claimed that the spread of Buddhism was the cause of an epidemic, the court reported this to the emperor. * {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} 15th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1587|en|1587|WD=Q6744|lt=1587}} {{ill|April 8|en|April 8|WD=Q2503|lt=April 8}}) – {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} set out with 25,000 troops to subjugate the Shimazu clan in Kyushu. * {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō}} 5th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1834|en|1834|WD=Q7591|lt=1834}} {{ill|April 9|en|April 9|WD=Q2504|lt=April 9}}) – {{ill|Mizuno Tadakuni|en|Mizuno Tadakuni|WD=Q2975848|lt=Mizuno Tadakuni}} assumed the office of {{ill|Rōjū|en|Rōjū|WD=Q1196812|lt=Rōjū}}, implementing the Tenpō reforms. * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji}} 4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871}} {{ill|April 20|en|April 20|WD=Q2524|lt=April 20}}) – Post offices (now known as {{ill|Post office|en|Post office|WD=Q35054|lt=Yūbin-kyoku}}) were established in Tokyo, Kyoto, and Osaka, marking the beginning of postal services (recognized as Postal Memorial Day).
== Births == * {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei}} 12nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1829|en|1829|WD=Q7572|lt=1829}} {{ill|April 4|en|April 4|WD=Q2508|lt=April 4}}) – {{ill|Inoue Genzaburō|en|Inoue Genzaburō|WD=Q1039195|lt=Inoue Genzaburō}}, a member of the Shinsengumi (died {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868}}). * {{ill|Genji era|en|Genji era|WD=Q1325419|lt=Genji}} 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1864|en|1864|WD=Q7704|lt=1864}} {{ill|April 6|en|April 6|WD=Q2506|lt=April 6}}) – {{ill|Wakamatsu Sentako|ja|若松賎子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamatsu Sentako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若松賤子|1=Wakamatsu Shizuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q535871}}, a translator (died {{ill|1896|en|1896|WD=Q7846|lt=1896}}). * {{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1867|en|1867|WD=Q7715|lt=1867}} {{ill|April 5|en|April 5|WD=Q2507|lt=April 5}}) – {{ill|Mutō Yamaichi|ja|武藤山治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutō Yamaichi|1=Mutō Yamaichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23998342}}, a businessperson and manager of the Kanebo conglomerate (died {{ill|1934|en|1934|WD=Q18714|lt=1934}}). * Meiji 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1867|en|1867|WD=Q7715|lt=1867}} {{ill|April 5|en|April 5|WD=Q2507|lt=April 5}}) – {{ill|Shimura Gentarō|ja|志村源太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimura Gentarō|1=Shimura Gentarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21699960}}, a banker and president of the Nippon Kangyo Bank (died {{ill|1930|en|1930|WD=Q18787|lt=1930}}).

== Deaths == * [[:ja:欽明天皇|欽明天皇]] 32nd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|570|en|570|WD=Q23885|lt=570}} {{ill|March 22|en|March 22|WD=Q2435|lt=March 22}}) – {{ill|Soga no Iname|en|Soga no Iname|WD=Q595897|lt=Soga Iname}}, a great minister (died ?; birth year may be around {{ill|506|en|506|WD=Q32909|lt=506}}). * [[:ja:弘仁|弘仁]] 8th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|817|en|817|WD=Q25030|lt=817}} {{ill|March 21|en|March 21|WD=Q2433|lt=March 21}}) – {{ill|Katsumichi|ja|勝道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsumichi|1=Shōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400506}}, the founder of Nikko-san (born {{ill|735|en|735|WD=Q23576|lt=735}}).
== Commemorations == * [[:ja:御船祭|御船祭]] – A festival held in Toba, Mie Prefecture, celebrating the sacred boat ritual.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 1 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月1日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 1일]][[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 1 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:三月初一]][[zh:三月初一]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 1 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月1日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 1일]][[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 1 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:三月初一]][[zh:三月初一]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 1 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月1日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 1일]][[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 1 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:三月初一]][[zh:三月初一]]
{{Not to be confused with|March 1|qq=March 1}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?-->
{{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初一|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 1일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 1''' is the 1st day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842|1=senpu}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:ja:敏達天皇|敏達天皇]] 14th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|585|en|585|WD=Q32201|lt=585|1=585}} {{ill|April 5|en|April 5|WD=Q2507|lt=April 5|1=April 5}}) – In response to an appeal by the anti-Buddhist advocate {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|1=Mononobe no Moriya}}, who claimed that the spread of Buddhism was the cause of an epidemic, the court reported this to the emperor. * {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 15th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1587|en|1587|WD=Q6744|lt=1587|1=1587}} {{ill|April 8|en|April 8|WD=Q2503|lt=April 8|1=April 8}}) – {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} set out with 25,000 troops to subjugate the Shimazu clan in Kyushu. * {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō|1=Tenpō}} 5th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1834|en|1834|WD=Q7591|lt=1834|1=1834}} {{ill|April 9|en|April 9|WD=Q2504|lt=April 9|1=April 9}}) – {{ill|Mizuno Tadakuni|en|Mizuno Tadakuni|WD=Q2975848|lt=Mizuno Tadakuni|1=Mizuno Tadakuni}} assumed the office of {{ill|Rōjū|en|Rōjū|WD=Q1196812|lt=Rōjū|1=Rōjū}}, implementing the Tenpō reforms. * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji|1=Meiji era}} 4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871|1=1871}} {{ill|April 20|en|April 20|WD=Q2524|lt=April 20|1=April 20}}) – Post offices (now known as {{ill|Post office|en|Post office|WD=Q35054|lt=Yūbin-kyoku|1=Post office}}) were established in Tokyo, Kyoto, and Osaka, marking the beginning of postal services (recognized as Postal Memorial Day).
== Births == * {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei|1=Bunsei}} 12nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1829|en|1829|WD=Q7572|lt=1829|1=1829}} {{ill|April 4|en|April 4|WD=Q2508|lt=April 4|1=April 4}}) – {{ill|Inoue Genzaburō|en|Inoue Genzaburō|WD=Q1039195|lt=Inoue Genzaburō|1=Inoue Genzaburō}}, a member of the Shinsengumi (died {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}}). * {{ill|Genji era|en|Genji era|WD=Q1325419|lt=Genji|1=Genji era}} 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1864|en|1864|WD=Q7704|lt=1864|1=1864}} {{ill|April 6|en|April 6|WD=Q2506|lt=April 6|1=April 6}}) – {{ill|Wakamatsu Sentako|ja|若松賎子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamatsu Sentako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若松賤子|1=Wakamatsu Shizuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q535871}}, a translator (died {{ill|1896|en|1896|WD=Q7846|lt=1896|1=1896}}). * {{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō|1=Keiō}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1867|en|1867|WD=Q7715|lt=1867|1=1867}} {{ill|April 5|en|April 5|WD=Q2507|lt=April 5|1=April 5}}) – {{ill|Mutō Yamaichi|ja|武藤山治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutō Yamaichi|1=Q23998342|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23998342}}, a businessperson and manager of the Kanebo conglomerate (died {{ill|1934|en|1934|WD=Q18714|lt=1934|1=1934}}). * Meiji 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1867|en|1867|WD=Q7715|lt=1867|1=1867}} {{ill|April 5|en|April 5|WD=Q2507|lt=April 5|1=April 5}}) – {{ill|Shimura Gentarō|ja|志村源太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimura Gentarō|1=Shimura Gentarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21699960}}, a banker and president of the Nippon Kangyo Bank (died {{ill|1930|en|1930|WD=Q18787|lt=1930|1=1930}}).
== Deaths == * [[:ja:欽明天皇|欽明天皇]] 32nd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|570|en|570|WD=Q23885|lt=570|1=570}} {{ill|March 22|en|March 22|WD=Q2435|lt=March 22|1=March 22}}) – {{ill|Soga no Iname|en|Soga no Iname|WD=Q595897|lt=Soga Iname|1=Soga no Iname}}, a great minister (died ?; birth year may be around {{ill|506|en|506|WD=Q32909|lt=506|1=506}}). * [[:ja:弘仁|弘仁]] 8th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|817|en|817|WD=Q25030|lt=817|1=817}} {{ill|March 21|en|March 21|WD=Q2433|lt=March 21|1=March 21}}) – {{ill|Katsumichi|ja|勝道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsumichi|1=Shōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400506}}, the founder of Nikko-san (born {{ill|735|en|735|WD=Q23576|lt=735|1=735}}).
== Commemorations == * [[:ja:御船祭|御船祭]] – A festival held in Toba, Mie Prefecture, celebrating the sacred boat ritual.
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|2|30|3|2|2|1|4|3|0301|qq=}}

Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842|1=senpu}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:ja:敏達天皇|敏達天皇]] 14th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|585|en|585|WD=Q32201|lt=585|1=585}} {{ill|April 5|en|April 5|WD=Q2507|lt=April 5|1=April 5}}) – In response to an appeal by the anti-Buddhist advocate {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|1=Mononobe no Moriya}}, who claimed that the spread of Buddhism was the cause of an epidemic, the court reported this to the emperor. * {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 15th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1587|en|1587|WD=Q6744|lt=1587|1=1587}} {{ill|April 8|en|April 8|WD=Q2503|lt=April 8|1=April 8}}) – {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} set out with 25,000 troops to subjugate the Shimazu clan in Kyushu. * {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō|1=Tenpō}} 5th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1834|en|1834|WD=Q7591|lt=1834|1=1834}} {{ill|April 9|en|April 9|WD=Q2504|lt=April 9|1=April 9}}) – {{ill|Mizuno Tadakuni|en|Mizuno Tadakuni|WD=Q2975848|lt=Mizuno Tadakuni|1=Mizuno Tadakuni}} assumed the office of {{ill|Rōjū|en|Rōjū|WD=Q1196812|lt=Rōjū|1=Rōjū}}, implementing the Tenpō reforms. * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji|1=Meiji era}} 4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871|1=1871}} {{ill|April 20|en|April 20|WD=Q2524|lt=April 20|1=April 20}}) – Post offices (now known as {{ill|Post office|en|Post office|WD=Q35054|lt=Yūbin-kyoku|1=Post office}}) were established in Tokyo, Kyoto, and Osaka, marking the beginning of postal services (recognized as Postal Memorial Day).
== Births == * {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei|1=Bunsei}} 12nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1829|en|1829|WD=Q7572|lt=1829|1=1829}} {{ill|April 4|en|April 4|WD=Q2508|lt=April 4|1=April 4}}) – {{ill|Inoue Genzaburō|en|Inoue Genzaburō|WD=Q1039195|lt=Inoue Genzaburō|1=Inoue Genzaburō}}, a member of the Shinsengumi (died {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}}). * {{ill|Genji era|en|Genji era|WD=Q1325419|lt=Genji|1=Genji era}} 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1864|en|1864|WD=Q7704|lt=1864|1=1864}} {{ill|April 6|en|April 6|WD=Q2506|lt=April 6|1=April 6}}) – {{ill|Wakamatsu Sentako|ja|若松賎子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamatsu Sentako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若松賤子|1=Wakamatsu Shizuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q535871}}, a translator (died {{ill|1896|en|1896|WD=Q7846|lt=1896|1=1896}}). * {{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō|1=Keiō}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1867|en|1867|WD=Q7715|lt=1867|1=1867}} {{ill|April 5|en|April 5|WD=Q2507|lt=April 5|1=April 5}}) – {{ill|Mutō Yamaichi|ja|武藤山治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutō Yamaichi|1=Q23998342|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23998342}}, a businessperson and manager of the Kanebo conglomerate (died {{ill|1934|en|1934|WD=Q18714|lt=1934|1=1934}}). * Meiji 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1867|en|1867|WD=Q7715|lt=1867|1=1867}} {{ill|April 5|en|April 5|WD=Q2507|lt=April 5|1=April 5}}) – {{ill|Shimura Gentarō|ja|志村源太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimura Gentarō|1=Shimura Gentarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21699960}}, a banker and president of the Nippon Kangyo Bank (died {{ill|1930|en|1930|WD=Q18787|lt=1930|1=1930}}).

== Commemorations == * [[:ja:御船祭|御船祭]] – A festival held in Toba, Mie Prefecture, celebrating the sacred boat ritual.

{{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初一|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 1일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 1''' is the 1st day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842|1=senpu}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:ja:敏達天皇|敏達天皇]] 14th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|585|en|585|WD=Q32201|lt=585|1=585}} {{ill|April 5|en|April 5|WD=Q2507|lt=April 5|1=April 5}}) – In response to an appeal by the anti-Buddhist advocate {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|1=Mononobe no Moriya}}, who claimed that the spread of Buddhism was the cause of an epidemic, the court reported this to the emperor. * {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 15th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1587|en|1587|WD=Q6744|lt=1587|1=1587}} {{ill|April 8|en|April 8|WD=Q2503|lt=April 8|1=April 8}}) – {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} set out with 25,000 troops to subjugate the Shimazu clan in Kyushu. * {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō|1=Tenpō}} 5th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1834|en|1834|WD=Q7591|lt=1834|1=1834}} {{ill|April 9|en|April 9|WD=Q2504|lt=April 9|1=April 9}}) – {{ill|Mizuno Tadakuni|en|Mizuno Tadakuni|WD=Q2975848|lt=Mizuno Tadakuni|1=Mizuno Tadakuni}} assumed the office of {{ill|Rōjū|en|Rōjū|WD=Q1196812|lt=Rōjū|1=Rōjū}}, implementing the Tenpō reforms. * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji|1=Meiji era}} 4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871|1=1871}} {{ill|April 20|en|April 20|WD=Q2524|lt=April 20|1=April 20}}) – Post offices (now known as {{ill|Post office|en|Post office|WD=Q35054|lt=Yūbin-kyoku|1=Post office}}) were established in Tokyo, Kyoto, and Osaka, marking the beginning of postal services (recognized as Postal Memorial Day).
== Births == * {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei|1=Bunsei}} 12nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1829|en|1829|WD=Q7572|lt=1829|1=1829}} {{ill|April 4|en|April 4|WD=Q2508|lt=April 4|1=April 4}}) – {{ill|Inoue Genzaburō|en|Inoue Genzaburō|WD=Q1039195|lt=Inoue Genzaburō|1=Inoue Genzaburō}}, a member of the Shinsengumi (died {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}}). * {{ill|Genji era|en|Genji era|WD=Q1325419|lt=Genji|1=Genji era}} 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1864|en|1864|WD=Q7704|lt=1864|1=1864}} {{ill|April 6|en|April 6|WD=Q2506|lt=April 6|1=April 6}}) – {{ill|Wakamatsu Sentako|ja|若松賎子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamatsu Sentako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若松賤子|1=Wakamatsu Shizuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q535871}}, a translator (died {{ill|1896|en|1896|WD=Q7846|lt=1896|1=1896}}). * {{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō|1=Keiō}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1867|en|1867|WD=Q7715|lt=1867|1=1867}} {{ill|April 5|en|April 5|WD=Q2507|lt=April 5|1=April 5}}) – {{ill|Mutō Yamaichi|ja|武藤山治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutō Yamaichi|1=Q23998342|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23998342}}, a businessperson and manager of the Kanebo conglomerate (died {{ill|1934|en|1934|WD=Q18714|lt=1934|1=1934}}). * Meiji 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1867|en|1867|WD=Q7715|lt=1867|1=1867}} {{ill|April 5|en|April 5|WD=Q2507|lt=April 5|1=April 5}}) – {{ill|Shimura Gentarō|ja|志村源太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimura Gentarō|1=Shimura Gentarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21699960}}, a banker and president of the Nippon Kangyo Bank (died {{ill|1930|en|1930|WD=Q18787|lt=1930|1=1930}}).
== Deaths == * [[:ja:欽明天皇|欽明天皇]] 32nd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|570|en|570|WD=Q23885|lt=570|1=570}} {{ill|March 22|en|March 22|WD=Q2435|lt=March 22|1=March 22}}) – {{ill|Soga no Iname|en|Soga no Iname|WD=Q595897|lt=Soga Iname|1=Soga no Iname}}, a great minister (died ?; birth year may be around {{ill|506|en|506|WD=Q32909|lt=506|1=506}}). * [[:ja:弘仁|弘仁]] 8th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|817|en|817|WD=Q25030|lt=817|1=817}} {{ill|March 21|en|March 21|WD=Q2433|lt=March 21|1=March 21}}) – {{ill|Katsumichi|ja|勝道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsumichi|1=Shōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400506}}, the founder of Nikko-san (born {{ill|735|en|735|WD=Q23576|lt=735|1=735}}).
== Commemorations == * [[:ja:御船祭|御船祭]] – A festival held in Toba, Mie Prefecture, celebrating the sacred boat ritual.
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|2|30|3|2|2|1|4|3|0301|qq=}}
